title: Hamnet Costume Design by Malgosia Turzanska: Oscar 2026 Nominees
text: Malgosia Turzanska earned her inaugural Academy Award nomination for costume design on Chloe Zhao's Hamnet, a historical fiction exploring how William Shakespeare found creative inspiration following his son's death. The costume design employs color as a narrative tool reflecting emotional journeys. Agnes, portrayed as a nature-connected healer, initially wears vibrant reds and oranges symbolizing vitality and connection to earth. Director Zhao intentionally selected red for its association with the root chakra. As the story progresses through marriage and motherhood, her palette shifts to rust tones, then evolves into purples, greys, and browns following her son's death hues evoking bruising and healing. Will's costumes utilize blues and greens, aligning him with water and representing the throat chakra governing creativity. Like Agnes, his clothing becomes progressively drained of color and vibrancy as events unfold. Turzanska incorporated historically informed details including pinking and slashing techniques executed with laser-cut leather. Will's slashes expand and become more pronounced throughout the narrative, eventually creating substantial negative space in the garments. The designer consulted Ruth Goodman's How To Be A Tudor and Citizen Portrait, featuring period artwork of ordinary people. Globe players wore Elizabethan-shaped garments constructed from raw linen treated with latex deliberately anachronistic choices bridging historical accuracy with contemporary visual language.
